name: textual-screen-push-vs-refresh-in-place
tags: textual tui screen stack push_screen navigation leak multiple entry points state machine task:e345785c
body:
In a Textual app (or similar screen-stack UI), a "land on screen X" action reachable from more than one entry point must check whether the current screen is already X (refresh in place) before deciding whether to push a new instance — unconditionally pushing leaks the screen stack, so 'back'/'escape' from the new screen returns to the stale old one instead of the true previous screen.

Why: found in tui/app.py (SeniorDevAgent) — load_db()/run_pass1() always did push_screen(ListScreen()), which was fine from the single entry point during initial dev, but broke once a second entry point (switching db while already inside ListScreen) was added.

How to apply: for any navigation helper that can be invoked from multiple call sites, branch on `isinstance(self.screen, TargetScreen)` — refresh in place if already there, push fresh only if not.
